templates.name: Native 4-agent Daily Thread
templates.description: Daily X thread on a trending topic via the xAI native multi-agent endpoint. Medium reasoning effort, 4 agents — the cost / quality sweet spot for day-to-day shipping.

templates.name: Native 16-agent Weekly Research
templates.description: Weekly deep-research thread. 16 agents at high reasoning effort — the setup Orchestra was designed for when the output has to be right. Auto-degrade on 429s.

templates.name: Truthseeker Debate
templates.description: Full visible Grok / Harper / Benjamin / Lucas debate over 3 rounds for a single fact-check. Harper searches; Benjamin runs code for numerical checks; Lucas hunts flaws.

templates.name: Hierarchical Research Report
templates.description: Two-team hierarchy — ResearchTeam (Harper + Benjamin) gathers, CritiqueTeam (Lucas + Grok) stress-tests, Grok synthesises into an X thread. Great for research-report-to-thread workflows.

templates.name: Dynamic Trend Analyzer
templates.description: Grok splits "biggest X trends today" into 3 sub-analyses; Harper + Lucas mini-debates run concurrently, Grok aggregates. Trades sequential rounds for fan-out parallelism.

templates.name: Policy Debate Loop
templates.description: Iterate up to 5 rounds toward a maximally-balanced 280-char summary of a contested topic; exits early on consensus. Mid-loop Lucas veto catches framing bias between iterations.

templates.name: Parallel-tools Fact Check
templates.description: Native 4-agent run with enforced per-agent tool routing — Harper owns the internet, Benjamin owns the sandbox, Lucas and Grok reason from the transcript. Off-list tool calls produce warnings.

templates.name: Resilient Native 16
templates.description: Native-16 run wrapped in the recovery pattern — on rate-limit or tool-execution errors, degrades to grok-4.20-0309 at medium effort and retries once. Zero overhead on the happy path.

templates.name: Combined Trendseeker (cron-ready)
templates.description: Flagship combined run — Bridge generates a lightweight X scraper module, Orchestra debates the scraped output, Lucas vetoes the synthesis, deploy. Built to be cron-scheduled.

templates.name: Combined Coder-Critic (TS CLI)
templates.description: Bridge generates a small TypeScript CLI; Orchestra runs a 4-role critique of the generated code itself; Lucas vetoes the review thread; deploy writes files + posts the review thread to X.

templates.name: Deep Research (Hierarchical 3-deep)
templates.description: Recursive sub-question generation, 3 levels deep, with a Lucas veto on every level synthesis. Built for research questions you can't fit in one prompt.

templates.name: Local-Docs Debate Loop
templates.description: Iteratively debate the contents of a local PDF/Markdown corpus until consensus. Requires v0.3+ for full local-docs ingest; earlier releases ignore `report_source: local`.

templates.name: Competitive Analysis
templates.description: Hierarchical competitor brief — Harper researches, Benjamin audits the logic, Grok synthesises, Lucas vetoes any claim without a named source.

templates.name: Investor Memo (DD)
templates.description: 1-pager investor memo — public sources only, hype-vetoed, ≥ 3 risks enforced. Lucas blocks output if Risks < 3 or any hype word survives review.

templates.name: Red-team the Plan
templates.description: Stress-test a plan from 4 angles in one round — no external research, no API key for `dry-run`. Great first run for newcomers; exercises every code path offline.

templates.name: Weekly News Digest
templates.description: Topic + ISO date range → bullet digest with inline citations and 2–3 named themes. Web search is full-fidelity in v0.3+.

templates.name: Paper Summarizer
templates.description: Structured arXiv / PDF summary — Problem, Method, Results, Limitations, What's next. Lucas vetoes results-paraphrasing that doesn't cite a specific table or figure.

templates.name: Product Launch Brief
templates.description: 5-section launch brief — positioning, audience, channels, risks (≥ 3 enforced), KPIs (≤ 5, measurable + cadence). Hype-vetoed positioning.
